item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there,

Sincerely doubt that this'll ever be allowed on the link page, but it's worth a shot. Me and my mates are trying to make an SCP RP Discord Server and I'm basically trying to advertsie it to places where people may be interested.

If it's seen as spam, feel free to delete

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure if this is really being maintained - but if it is, we should probably add in the Portuguese and Ukrainian branches, no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TheDuckman's tumblr is NSFW, maybe it should be flagged as such on the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone can check me on this, but if I recall correctly, due to the CI wiki not having quality standards similar to those on the mainsite, it's not officially affiliated. I haven't seen the state of that wiki recently though.

I would add that many of the sites listed on this page are not related to the SCP Foundation.

Those are "further reading" sections, which are recommendations for people who enjoyed the work on the SCP wiki and would like to explore other similar things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Sarroze. Can we have an answer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Up.

I would add that many of the sites listed on this page are not related to the SCP Foundation.

PS: Sorry for my bad English, I'm French

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi!

I just have a little question about this page.
Why not add the site of the Chaos Insurgency? It is a site of writing related to the Foundation which is, I think, well done, quite active, there are even branches of translation (spanish and chinese) and with original content. I think that it really has its place in the list of sites related to the SCP Foundation.

Thanks and have a good day!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whenever this topic comes up, I always recommend Flickr, which has a giant catalog of "ordinary" photos, and excellent license filters.

I don't know about adding it in here. It seems overly broad compared to the rest of the links on the page, which are either directly connected to scp-wiki, or are related to creepypasta in general in some way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good idea. I think there's a thread in ideas and brainstorming about an image gallery in wikipedia with interesting images… I'll pass this on to our Image Team.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps this page could link to some good sources of CC-compliant images? The Visual Records wiki is nice, but it's A) copyright-dubious and B) focused more on the fantastical or visually unusual, when many pieces are served better by more mundane imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Holders link (theholders.org) seems to be dead. The Internet Archive has a snapshot here: https://web.archive.org/web/20160710213713/http://theholders.org/

Edit: Seems to be back now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How to get a new branch acknowledged is described here.

If you are serious about this i suggest talking to the Internet Outreach Team, especially Roget.

If you need tips from another branches Admin how to set up stuff, you can also ask me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The right place to ask would have been in the Policy subforum, but in order for an SCP translation site to be acknowledged/affiliated with the mainsite, it needs a few things; if I recall correctly, it needs a decently-sized and active community and unique articles specific to that site written by said community, as well as translations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How do someone add a new branch for other language?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Forgot to leave a comment in the history. Removed a link to a broken page that's no longer applicable or affiliated with the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god, I never even noticed that. (Translation: I was never informed of that change :L) Welp, that's good then. I'll be working on moving the SCD's server over to a more stable host atm anyway. I'll see if I can edit the link on the Chat-guide to the new page when it's moved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Chat guide page has a link to the stats page on the tab Chatbot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My opinion only, but maybe the chat stats could be on mentioned on the chat-guide.

I think stats does deserve a page somewhere, and not just a forum post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if it's relevant/should be added yet, but I have two things:
http://scp.so/ircstats ( OR http://thebes.openshells.net/~fiestasheep/SCPStats.html ) is a statistics page containing statistics for the #Site19 channel.

I'm also working on a little project similiar to Anqxyr's Yet Another Statistics Thread. I'm aiming to have all the features that Anqxyr has there, except it'd update frequently (some features like ratings, edits, etc. being updated hourly.) and resides on it's own site. I already have a skeleton of the site setup (http://thebes.openshells.net/~fiestasheep it's down ATM due to DDOSing kids)

Maybe something to look into for the future to be added when It's finished/functional? :L

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Broken link removed. Thanks for the notification!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can someone remove the link to Obakemono? It appears to no longer exist as a site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: History, mostly. They've up since almost the beginning. And since there's no reason to take them down, they stick around until someone wants to do something with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the foundation fronts aren't being used why are they there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you wonder what type of community the workers of The Foundation, the Chaos Insurgency live in, it's probably something like Night Vale - where they have their own twice monthly community radio podcast.

…but you should never speak of it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is http://www.scp-foundation.com/ dead (unchanged in two years), or is it my imagination?

Also, there seems to be no mention of http://scparchive.wikidot.com/ which contains the old PDFs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'm with Tombs. Maybe we should sidebar links for the translation sites, like we have for the Admin Site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I agree. Before all the social media pages got revamped, I was fine with this the way it was. It's just that everyone on IO's busted their tails trying to get the social media up to snuff, and I think they're presentable and active enough now to warrant more attention and promotion on the site than they currently have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds good to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm on Community Outreach, and we manage the front page (maybe? in theory?) and I support this. Perhaps it didn't make sense at the time, but with the more organized/unified approach to the social media sites of the past half-year or so, it definitely deserves the attention now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like that. It's not like we have a finite homepage.

Also I think social media sites are more important than links to translation sites, because 1) most people visiting the wiki would probably be more interested in following the wiki somewhere they already frequent, than visiting an off-shot site in a language they may not even speak; and 2) people who would want to visit the translation site are more than likely found out about us from them in the first place, and thus already know where to find them.

(This is not me arguing that translation sites should be removed from the main page. This is me arguing that since they are on the main page, social media sites should be there as well.)

Also I usually get to our social media pages through these links, and it always takes me forever to navigate to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a member of IO, I still say that the links to social media sites will be better served directly on the homepage instead of buried here in the bottom of a more or less unseen page of the site with an otherwise unassuming label on the top/sidebars leading to them.

Can I get some thoughts on this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems that the Josef K Stories site has been moved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sure no one here will be interested in trying to win a free Advanced Reader’s Copy (ARC) of Lovecraft’s Monsters.

Really, no one will need this link.

http://tachyonpub.tumblr.com/post/76635238312/were-giving-away-lovecrafts-monsters

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: added link SCP-TH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Josef K. Stories site has been moved and now links to a site that redirects to the new one. Shall we update our link or leave it as is?

E: Hey, they've linked us too! Awesomeness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's linked in the translation box at the bottom of the main page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …This is probably worth mentioning on the Site News. Who does that, again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added link to SCP-PL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I added SCP-RU and SCP-Korea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Them having legal permission doesn't make them any less assholes from swiping it from under us.

In the same way, someone who sets his company up such that his employees work plenty of unpaid overtime just to keep their jobs is quite often within his legal rights, but it doesn't make him not a swine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They have permission. That's what Creative Commons is for. Read it.

I couldn't find where on that page it says "but you have to ask for a second permission or we will so totally not like you"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The chinese are translating it and so are the Russians. If they claimed authorship to the stuff, you'd maybe have a leg to stand on, but as of present, I don't see the problem.

Giving it a quick read, they don't actually seem to be doing that. Only things in common I see are MC&D and being in the same world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If anything, Holders are a good example why we have voting and deletion procedures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the Holders, x and the defunct Creepypasta.net are on this list, i really dont see why the goons MCD site shouldn't be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The chinese are translating it and so are the Russians. If they claimed authorship to the stuff, you'd maybe have a leg to stand on, but as of present, I don't see the problem.
Especially since a forumboard with a translation does nothing for us, while making up the MC&D wiki at the most obvious URL possible means we can't ever have our own.

And it matters on principle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dude!

The Chinese, in fact, are copying actual content without permission. I'm not sure if I recall correctly but I think the Russian site appeared well before we ever saw our faithful translator here. (Could be wrong, my memory is obviously for shit.)

Besides, if you're right that it's dead on the vine, who cares?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, thought you were Staff. I must need caffeine…

Bwhahahahah epic fail.

I'm not stressed about them not being "official" - I'm planning on adding a link to the Chinese version also, and there isn't a need to ask for permission on things.

I don't see how this is relevant at all.

You may be pissed about them (in fairness, while I'm not, you're not the only one) but that doesn't make them go away. I'd rather not have a war about it - plus under our CC license they're actually in the right.

No. What they are doing is perfectly legal under our license, which is the reason why it hasn't ended in a takedown notice. However, they aren't right - it's still douchebaggery.
And I don't care if it makes them go away or not - the question is whether we really need to endorse them and advertise them here.
Hell, in this way ,you might as well link the Homestuck SCPs failthread from MSPA forums. In fact ,although it's patently stupid, I like it more because they at least didn't swipe anything from under our legs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, thought you were Staff. I must need caffeine…

I'm not stressed about them not being "official" - I'm planning on adding a link to the Chinese version also, and there isn't a need to ask for permission on things.

You may be pissed about them (in fairness, while I'm not, you're not the only one) but that doesn't make them go away. I'd rather not have a war about it - plus under our CC license they're actually in the right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) It would have been simpler to say "Note: VAElynx isn't staff.", which, for the readers, is completely true. Your statement actually makes me think that there are two people, one staff and one not.

2) To the merit of the case - the thing that pisses me off is that they swiped the MC&D wiki without a word, and worse than that, after posting what, eight short articles, noone gives an arse anymore, and so it lies dead and rotting.
I see zero reason why we should essentially advertise their site here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: -double post-

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's only "MOSTLY" dead… And I suspect that it's someone that thought it would be "cool" to create an account just for that purpose. (Yes, not too bright.)

But 75% of the other links on that page are from non-members to other sites. And it's actually seen more recent activity then the GOC wiki.

It's not like I posted these:

  • [http://corpsesoap-wiki.wikidot.com] Dr Gerald (Front - Ancillary - Old)
  • [http://scp-069-j.wikidot.com] Raven Mackenzie (Script-Sisters of Cheyenne Point - Old)
  • [http://scp-application.wikidot.com] The Administrator (Application site - Old)
  • [http://scp-wiki-admin.wikidot.com] The Administrator (SCP Admin Court of Counsel - Old)

Still not sure this one was supposed to be ours or not:

  • [http://thefactory.wikidot.com] ???? (Unknown)

There are also others that AREN'T mentioned.

Serpents Hand is owned by yoric, actually, unless he's transferred MA to someone else.

(Clarification: This is a discussion with the user VAElynx not an argument with Senior Staffer VAElynx.)

Further, this took about five minutes to post, so WikiDot is still failing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Serpent's hand is maintained by I think Mann and it's active
MC&D is a pretty dead wiki maintained by a bunch of Goons that aren't even members of this site.

See the difference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you want to be literal, none of them NEED to be here. But it's probably as relevant as the Serpents Hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does the MC&D wiki really need to be here, Bland?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added some! Thanks again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm - also if you're not only interested in horror:

Baen (and some other publishers) have part of their catalog available for free here:
http://www.baen.com/library/
and here
http://www.webscription.net/c-1-free-library.aspx

And speaking of Baen, some of you know that they release CDs occasionally with their hardcover books.
The files are legally available for free here:
http://baencd.thefifthimperium.com/

(I should say there is SOME horror sprinkled in there, but most of that is "Dark Fantasy" so don't expect a lot of creepypasta.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added and thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If (like me) you spend a decent amount of time traveling, podcasted books are quite useful. And free ones are VERY useful!

http://www.podiobooks.com/podiobooks/search.php?category=11

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This page is now being updated and maintained. If anyone has any suggestions or links they'd like added, please add them to the discussion page, and we'll jump all over it.
